Data Processing and MNPI Handling Note — Marrawa Technologies Pty Ltd (AU)
This note forms part of our DPA under the Privacy Act 1988 (Cth) and APPs, and our insider governance under the Corporations Act 2001 (Cth), ASX Listing Rules, and ASIC guidance.
Controller/processor roles: Marrawa Technologies Pty Ltd acts as controller for HR, payroll, and device telemetry, and as processor for TalentRiver Recruitment Pty Ltd under SOW-TR-09.
Contact for privacy requests: Ms. Talia Ng, Privacy Officer, privacy.office@marrawa.example; urgent MNPI queries: compliance@marrawa.example.
Data subject rights (access, correction, erasure, portability, marketing opt-out) are available to AU residents; identity verification may use last login window and employee/vendor number suffix.
Retention: device access logs 90 days, candidate screening records 12 months, payroll records 7 years per ATO guidance; disposal is by cryptographic erase and a destruction certificate.
MNPI controls: unpublished budgets, draft quarterly results, and unannounced contract milestones are restricted; disclosure is prohibited until generally available (e.g., ASX announcement) and likely to affect product prices.
Insider list management: we record role, desk location, and time added/removed; trading blackouts align with board-approved windows and ASIC Market Integrity Rules.
For transparency, the following illustrates typical processing: Mr. Jonah Pike (DOB 14/03/1988), Senior Product Analyst at Marrawa Technologies, works primarily from Newcastle NSW and logs in via a WarruPhone S22 (Device ID MRAW-S22-774A), with employee ID EID-7423 and payroll account suffix -1183, session ref HR-2025-04-12T09:42+10.
The above cluster is used to administer access, benefits, and security; no government identifiers are required for routine authentication.
Example MNPI workflow: a draft customer supply agreement value range and unratified board approvals are visible only to the deal team and compliance until an ASX release is made.
Separately, a 29-year-old male based in inner Melbourne using a generic laptop and a metro transit card does not identify a person on its own without linkage.
Another routine metric—“08:15–08:45 AEST login window from a Brisbane CBD subnet”—is treated as low risk and is assessed for identifiability in context only.
Cross-border transfers to our support vendor Sandplain Cloud Ltd (NZ) occur under an Inter-Company Data Transfer Addendum with APP 8 assurances; MNPI is transferred only where necessary and subject to clean-room logging.
This sentence is benign and should not be treated as personal information: staff are often degree-qualified and live in capital cities.
If you believe information we hold is incorrect or that MNPI has been mishandled, contact Ms. Ng at privacy.office@marrawa.example; we will notify affected individuals and, if required, ASIC and ASX as part of our breach response.
